name: Ancient & Medieval History
description: Ancient civilizations, classical mythology, and the Middle Ages

questions.q: What language was used across the Roman Empire for administration and the classical arts?

source.notes: Classical Latin evolved into the Romance languages.

questions.q: Roman aqueducts were engineered primarily to transport what substance into cities?

source.notes: Over 11 aqueducts supplied Rome itself; many sections still stand today.

questions.q: In Greek mythology, which mountain was home to the gods?

source.notes: A real mountain in northern Greece, 2,918 metres high.

questions.q: On which Greek island did the mythical Minotaur supposedly live in a labyrinth?

source.notes: The labyrinth is said to have been built by Daedalus for King Minos.

questions.q: Queen Boudica (Boadicea) led a revolt against Roman occupation of which ancient land?

source.notes: Queen of the Iceni tribe, revolted against the Romans around AD 60–61.

questions.q: Which Greek mathematician is famously associated with a theorem about right-angled triangles?

source.notes: a² + b² = c² in a right-angled triangle.

questions.q: Hieroglyphs were the pictorial writing system of which ancient civilization?

source.notes: Decoded via the Rosetta Stone, discovered in 1799.

questions.q: In Greek myth, the Trojan Horse was used in a war between the Greeks and which city?

source.notes: Troy was located in modern-day northwestern Turkey.

questions.q: Who is commonly regarded as the first Roman emperor?

source.notes: Born Gaius Octavius; reigned 27 BC – AD 14.

questions.q: The eruption of Mount Vesuvius in AD 79 buried which Roman city?

source.notes: Herculaneum was also buried in the same eruption.

questions.q: Roman general Mark Antony had a famous political and romantic alliance with which Egyptian ruler?

source.notes: Cleopatra VII; both committed suicide in 30 BC after losing to Octavian.

questions.q: In Greek myth, the abduction of Helen, Queen of Sparta, triggered which legendary conflict?

source.notes: Paris of Troy took Helen, sparking a 10-year siege.

questions.q: The powerful Medici family, central to the Renaissance, ruled which Italian city?

source.notes: Their patronage helped fund Michelangelo, Donatello, and Botticelli.

questions.q: Who was the notorious Roman emperor said to have ordered the murder of his own mother Agrippina?

source.notes: Nero ruled 54–68 AD; infamous for cruelty and the great fire of Rome.

questions.q: In Greek myth, which three-headed dog guarded the entrance to the underworld?

source.notes: Cerberus was tamed by Heracles as his twelfth labor.

questions.q: Ashoka the Great, a 3rd-century BC ruler famous for converting to Buddhism, ruled which subcontinent?

source.notes: Third emperor of the Mauryan Dynasty; ruled c. 268–232 BC.

questions.q: Hannibal, who famously crossed the Alps with war elephants, was a general of which empire?

source.notes: Led Carthaginian forces during the Second Punic War (218–201 BC).

questions.q: In ancient Rome, which color dye was reserved by law for garments worn only by the emperor?

source.notes: Tyrian purple was made from murex sea snails; extremely expensive.

questions.q: From which Northern European region did the Vikings originate?

source.notes: Primarily what is now Norway, Sweden, and Denmark; active roughly 793–1066 AD.

questions.q: The ancient city of Babylon was located in which present-day country?

source.notes: About 85 km south of Baghdad, near the Euphrates.

questions.q: Darius I ruled which vast ancient empire, stretching from the Indus to the Balkans?

source.notes: Reigned 522–486 BC; built Persepolis.

questions.q: Which Egyptian god, depicted with the head of a jackal, served as guide and protector of souls in the afterlife?

source.notes: Anubis oversaw mummification and the weighing of hearts.
